name: Kurukh

ancestors.notes: Descent per Glottolog classification of Kurukh (kuru1301).

notes: Skeleton. Kurukh (Oraon), a North Dravidian language of eastern India written in Devanagari. Consonant letters and vowel signs (matras) are mapped to standard Devanagari IPA values, including the retroflex and breathy-voiced series and nuqta letters ड़ ढ़ ख़. The abugida's inherent schwa after bare consonants (and its deletion by virama or context) is NOT modeled here, so unpointed consonant sequences under-recover vowels and a high PER floor is expected. Contrastive vowel length and nasalization are only partly written, and word accent is unwritten; all are left unencoded beyond what the script marks.

sources.author: Hammarström, H. et al. (Glottolog 5.0)
sources.title: Glottolog: Kurukh (kuru1301)
sources.publisher: Max Planck Institute for Evolutionary Anthropology
